sourceMovie.title: Eli
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Eli works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Insidious
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eli clicked for you, Insidious (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eli?
faq.answer: Try Insidious, The Others, Doctor Sleep—each shares DNA with Eli while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eli?
faq.answer: Eli (2019) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eli worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
